messages.content: True or false? Follower count is often considered to be a vanity metric
True.

Why should you start planning the structure of your team by looking at business goals?

To help you determine what channels and content you’ll need and the staffing to make it happen.

To know if advertising is a priority so you can allocate enough funds.

To satisfy company stakeholders.

To understand if roadblocks such as lack of time and lack of people are obstacles that you’ll need to overcome.

The first option is correct: To help you determine what channels and content you’ll need and the staffing to make it happen. By starting with business goals, you can align your team's structure, roles, and responsibilities around what needs to be accomplished. This will help you ensure that the right people are in the right positions and that resources are allocated effectively to achieve the desired outcomes.

Which of the following steps is NOT one you need to think about when planning how to structure your social team?

Plan your business and social goals one to three years out.

Consider your stakeholders’ needs.

Allocate or advocate for resources.

Gather industry data to support your goal.
None of the above steps should be ignored when planning how to structure your social team. Each of them is a crucial element of the planning process.

The following are all ways that social media monitoring can benefit your business, except:

Supporting existing customers by answering questions about your product or service

Listening and centralizing social conversations about an ongoing marketing campaign

Measuring the performance of your latest email marketing campaign

informing your recruiting efforts to help align job descriptions with candidate expectations

Measuring the performance of your latest email marketing campaign is the option that doesn't relate to how social media monitoring can benefit your business. Social media monitoring refers to the process of listening to social media channels in order to discover, gather, and analyze relevant content related to your brand and industry. It can help support existing customers, listen and centralize social conversations, and inform your recruitment efforts by gaining insights into job candidates' expectations.

What should you do if your competitor experiences a big, public blunder?

Release the hounds! Your sales reps will love this opportunity.

Be empathetic and helpful towards your competitor’s customers.

Take the opportunity to share with your customers how this would never happen to you, or to your customers.

Do nothing. Eye on the prize!

The correct answer is "Be empathetic and helpful towards your competitor’s customers." When a competitor experiences a big, public blunder, it's not a good idea to take advantage of the situation by bashing them or their product. Instead, it's important to demonstrate empathy and offer helpful solutions to their affected customers. This will help build trust in your brand and show that you prioritize customer well-being over business competition. Stooping to negative tactics can backfire and end up damaging your own brand's reputation.

Why are animated GIFs useful on social media?

They can help you capture special metrics.

They can help you demonstrate complex concepts quickly.

They disappear after an hour so that no one will see them again.

People would rather see an animated GIF about a product than watch a video.

The correct answer is "They can help you demonstrate complex concepts quickly." Animated GIFs are useful on social media because they can convey emotions and complex ideas in a quick and eye-catching way. They're also easy to create and share, making them a popular choice for social media content. However, they do not capture any special metrics and they do not disappear after an hour. Additionally, while people may enjoy seeing animated GIFs, they are not necessarily preferred over video as a format for product demonstrations.
True or false? One in four consumers lose interest in a company if it doesn’t use video on its social media channels.
True.

Let’s say you conduct an audit of your existing social media content and find that on Twitter, posts with statistics get by far the most likes and retweets. What’s the BEST way for you to use this knowledge to inform your future social content plan?

Plan to scale back on your statistics posts on Twitter to make room for other posts that might perform better.

Plan to publish the same number of statistics posts on Twitter each week as you are already, but remove other post types since they clearly aren’t performing as well.

Plan to dramatically increase the number of statistics posts on Twitter so that statistics posts comprise at least 80% of the feed.

Plan to experiment with publishing more statistics to Twitter and perhaps investing time in doing more original research you can pull stats from and publish to Twitter and other channels.

The best way to use this knowledge to inform your future social content plan is to plan to experiment with publishing more statistics to Twitter and perhaps investing time in doing more original research you can pull stats from and publish to Twitter and other channels. It's important to take advantage of the insights generated from the audit to make informed decisions about optimizing your social media presence. In this case, it's clear that statistics posts are resonating well with your audience on Twitter, so it makes sense to continue publishing those types of content. However, it's also important to experiment with new content types to see if there are other formats that can also perform well. Therefore, it's important to strike a balance between continuing a successful strategy and experimenting with new tactics.

Your team is creating a social campaign for an upcoming product launch. Someone designs a great image for this social campaign that does a fantastic job of showcasing the product. How can your team optimize this image for the campaign?

Publish the same exact image to every social platform.

Create multiple versions of the same image that comply with each social channel’s ideal image dimensions.

The correct answer is: "Create multiple versions of the same image that comply with each social channel’s ideal image dimensions." This will ensure that the image is optimized for each social media platform and displays properly without cropping or distortion. Each social media platform has its own ideal dimensions for images. Therefore, creating multiple versions of the same image that comply with each social channel’s recommended dimensions will help ensure that the image looks its best across all platforms and will help increase engagement and reach for the social campaign.

Language like ____________ can help improve your clickthrough and conversion rates on social media because language makes the call-to-action seem more time sensitive, which can encourage instant action.

“Shop Now” or “Download Now”

“Learn More” or “Get More Info”

“Get Yours Free” or “Free Offer”

“Today Only” or “Ends at Midnight”

The correct answer is: “Today Only” or “Ends at Midnight”. The language "Today Only" or "Ends at Midnight" creates a sense of urgency, which can encourage followers to take immediate action. This can help improve your clickthrough and conversion rates on social media because followers are motivated to act before the time-limited opportunity expires. The other options listed can also be effective call-to-action phrases, but they do not create the same sense of urgency as "Today Only" or "Ends at Midnight".
True or false? Influencer marketing and user-generated content will help you bypass some social media platform rules to help you reach your audience.

False. Influencer marketing and user-generated content do not allow you to bypass social media platform rules. All content posted on social media platforms must comply with the platform's community guidelines and terms of service. Violating these rules can result in penalties such as being banned from the platform or having your content removed. Influencer marketing and user-generated content are effective strategies for reaching your audience, but they must be executed in accordance with the platform's rules and regulations.
True or false? Working with journalists and media personalities is just like working with other influencers.

False. Working with journalists and media personalities is not the same as working with other influencers. Journalists and media personalities have a different approach to content creation and a different set of skills and expertise compared to traditional influencers. While influencers are often recognized for their large social media followings, journalists and media personalities have built their reputation by providing high-quality content and reporting. When working with journalists and media personalities, it's important to understand their unique needs and priorities. Additionally, they may require different types of content and messaging compared to traditional influencers, so it's important to tailor your approach accordingly.
